original_hypothesis.title: ERC20 recovery in BlackClaims can drain active season reward token balance, breaking claims solvency
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: BlackClaims.recoverERC20() allows the owner/secondOwner to transfer the full balance of any ERC20 held by the contract, without excluding the reward token used for seasonal payouts. This can make the contract insolvent after finalize() pulls rewards from treasury, causing legitimate claimers to revert or receive reduced staking ability.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is an explicit unrestricted sweep function in a contract that holds user-claimable rewards. Even if intended for admin rescue, it directly violates reward solvency invariants and is a common real-world failure mode after key compromise or operational mistake.

proof_of_signal.pattern: recoverERC20(tokenAddress_) transfers full IERC20(tokenAddress_).balanceOf(this) to msg.sender with no restriction on reward token

preconditions: Contract has been funded via finalize() (reward token balance > 0)
Claims period is active or future claims expected

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: admin token sweep with no allowlist/denylist
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: reward token can be swept, leaving season.remaining_reward_amount > actual balance
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: no restriction `tokenAddress_ != address(token)` and no solvency post-condition

analysis_questions: Is recoverERC20 intended to exclude BLACK reward token? (documentation vs code mismatch)
Can sweep occur during active claim window causing permanent user loss?
Are there off-chain controls (multisig) assumed for owner that reduce realism?
validation_requirements: Unit test: finalize -> recoverERC20(rewardToken) -> user claimAndStakeReward reverts or underpays
Check if any other function relies on token balance invariants

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: If the BlackClaims owner key is compromised (or owner behaves maliciously), attacker calls recoverERC20(BLACK) to drain the reward pool, then users cannot claim/stake seasonal rewards despite having reported entitlements.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Loss of seasonal reward pool for all users in that season
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Requires refunding from treasury/admin; not enforceable on-chain

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: Likely missing negative test preventing sweep of reward token

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: Track admin sweeps and compare to season.remaining_reward_amount

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
